title: oreocarya interrupta
intro: Oreocarya interrupta, a plant species also known as mountain calysso or interrupted oreocarya, is a flowering plant in the borage family (Boraginaceae). It has been found to possess medicinal properties, particularly anti-inflammatory and antioxidant compounds, which contribute to its health benefits. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various ailments, including fever, cough, and respiratory issues, and is also believed to have antimicrobial properties. Oreocarya interrupta is typically prepared as an infusion or tincture for herbal remedies, with its leaves and stems being used in traditional medicine.
